title: Sales Tax, Accounting, and Keeping the Books
objectives: Explain Florida sales tax collection and remittance requirements
Describe basic U.S. bookkeeping expectations and why they differ from home-country practices
Estimate annual accounting costs for a small foreign-owned business

contentBlocks.title: Florida Sales Tax — It's Not Optional
contentBlocks.content:
Did you know that one founder owed $3,800 in penalties because she did not register for a free certificate before collecting sales tax? The rules are simple once you know them, but the cost of not knowing is steep.

Building on the federal and state income tax concepts from earlier in this module, sales tax adds another layer to your compliance obligations. If you sell tangible goods in Florida, you must collect sales tax. The base state rate is 6%, and most counties add a surtax of 0.5% to 1.5%. In Orange County (Orlando), the combined rate is 6.5%. In Osceola County, it is 7.5%. The rate depends on where the sale happens, not where your business is located.

Here is the practical takeaway. An Argentine founder selling leather goods from Orlando assumed sales tax only applied to big retailers. After nine months, the Florida Department of Revenue sent her a notice: $3,800 in uncollected sales tax plus penalties, paid out of pocket.

Before collecting sales tax, register for a Sales Tax Certificate with the Florida Department of Revenue : free, done online. Once registered, collect the correct rate on every taxable sale and remit to the state monthly, quarterly, or annually depending on volume.

Not everything is taxable. Most services and groceries (unprepared food) are exempt. Prepared food, clothing, electronics, and most physical products are taxable. Software and SaaS fall into a gray area — check with a CPA if you're selling digital products.

contentBlocks.title: How U.S. Sales Tax Compares to VAT — What You Already Know
contentBlocks.content:
If you're coming from almost any country outside the U.S., you're familiar with a Value Added Tax (VAT) system. Here's the critical difference: VAT is a national, centralized tax applied at every stage of production. U.S. sales tax is a state-level, point-of-sale tax — and every state makes its own rules.

What does that mean for you? VAT (what you know): One national rate. Applied at each stage of the supply chain. Businesses collect VAT on sales and claim credits for VAT paid on purchases. Registration thresholds and rates are set nationally.

U.S. Sales Tax (what you need to learn): No federal sales tax exists. Each state sets its own rate (or has no sales tax at all — Oregon, Montana, Delaware, New Hampshire charge zero). Counties and cities can add surtax. You only collect tax where you have "nexus" (a physical or economic connection to that state). There is no input credit system — you collect from the customer and remit to the state.

If you sell in multiple states, you register, collect, and remit in each one separately. A Colombian SaaS founder selling in Florida, Texas, and California spent $1,200 on a compliance service to handle multi-state filing.

The biggest adjustment: There is no single government portal for U.S. sales tax. You deal with each state individually. Services like Avalara or TaxJar can automate multi-state compliance. VAT is one relationship with one government. U.S. sales tax can be dozens.

contentBlocks.title: Collecting Sales Tax Without a Permit
contentBlocks.content: Some founders start collecting sales tax before registering with the Florida Department of Revenue — that's a compliance violation. Others don't collect at all and absorb the cost themselves — that's a growing liability. The correct sequence: register first, then collect. Registration is free and takes about 10 minutes online. Don't skip this step.

contentBlocks.title: U.S. Bookkeeping Expectations — Why It Matters More Here
contentBlocks.content:
In the U.S., the IRS expects your records to support every number on your tax return. Banks want clean financial statements before extending credit. Investors will ask for profit-and-loss statements. Even landlords may review business financials before signing a commercial lease.

The bottom line? The standard is GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles). For small businesses: separate business and personal finances, record all income and expenses, reconcile bank statements monthly, and keep receipts for at least three years (seven years for some records).

A food truck founder from Peru kept receipts in a shoebox. When his CPA asked for quarterly expense reports, he spent an entire weekend matching paper receipts to bank transactions. QuickBooks from day one would have taken five minutes.

The tools are affordable. QuickBooks Online ($30–$60/month) is one of the most widely used. Xero ($15–$78/month) is popular with international founders for its multi-currency support. Wave is free for basic invoicing and accounting. Pick one before your first sale and connect it to your bank account.

contentBlocks.title: Annual Accounting Costs — What to Budget

rows: QuickBooks Online, Xero, or Wave (free tier available)
Higher end for international clients with treaty filings, FBAR, or multi-state returns
Avalara or TaxJar for multi-state automation
Gusto, ADP Run, or Paychex. Handles withholding and filings.
If you don't want to do it yourself. Bench, Pilot, or local firms.

contentBlocks.title: Before You Hire an Accountant
contentBlocks.content:
Not all CPAs are created equal — and not all of them work with foreign-owned businesses. Before you hire an accountant, ask these questions:

  • Do you have experience filing returns for non-resident aliens or foreign-owned LLCs?
  • Are you familiar with tax treaty provisions for my home country?
  • Can you handle FBAR (Foreign Bank Account Report) filings?
  • Do you charge flat fees or hourly? (Flat fee is almost always better for small businesses.)
  • Can you help with multi-state sales tax compliance?

The SBDC (Small Business Development Center) in Orlando can refer you to CPAs who work with international founders. This is a free service.

contentBlocks.title: Check Your Understanding: Sales Tax and Bookkeeping

questions.stem: What is the most important difference between a VAT system and U.S. sales tax for an international founder to understand?
options: VAT rates are always higher than U.S. sales tax rates
U.S. sales tax is managed state-by-state with no federal sales tax, while VAT is typically one national system
U.S. sales tax applies only to services, while VAT applies only to goods
VAT is collected at the point of sale, while U.S. sales tax is collected at manufacturing
questions.explanation: The core difference is structural: VAT is a national, centralized system. U.S. sales tax is decentralized : each state sets its own rules, rates, and exemptions. There is no federal sales tax. This means founders selling in multiple states may need to register and file separately in each one.

questions.stem: A founder from Argentina has been selling leather goods online from Orlando for nine months without registering for a Florida Sales Tax Certificate. What is the most likely consequence?
options: Nothing — sales tax only applies to businesses with over $100,000 in revenue
She will receive a notice from the Florida Department of Revenue for uncollected sales tax plus penalties, which she must pay out of pocket
The IRS will revoke her EIN and close her business
Her customers will be individually liable for the unpaid tax
questions.explanation: The lesson describes this exact scenario: an Argentine founder owed $3,800 in uncollected sales tax plus penalties after nine months. Sales tax should have been collected from customers from the start. Without registration, the founder becomes personally liable for the tax that should have been collected. Registration is free and takes about 10 minutes.

contentBlocks.title: Reflection: Your Bookkeeping Plan
contentBlocks.prompt: Does your product or service trigger sales tax obligations in Florida? If you're selling in multiple U.S. states, which ones? Write down the bookkeeping tool you plan to use and when you'll set it up relative to your first U.S. sale.

contentBlocks.title: Key Takeaway
contentBlocks.content: Sales tax compliance is mandatory and easy to mess up. Register with the Florida Department of Revenue before collecting tax. U.S. sales tax works nothing like VAT — it's state-by-state with no input credits. Budget for a CPA who understands international clients. The cost of professional accounting ($500–$2,500/year) is far less than the cost of IRS penalties and state compliance violations.
